contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Phase 4 spec — credential freshness (3 sources, web-vs-PDF doctrine)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/152
  
  Details:
  Closed the design loop on credential page freshness. The page today only reflects Ledger history (quarterly canonical settlement), so contributors logging work this week don't see it for ~3 months. Phase 4 adds two parallel sources alongside, with a clean web-vs-PDF doctrine.
  
  CREDENTIALING_PROGRAM_PAGES.md §18 ships the spec; implementation deferred to sub-phases 4.1-4.4.
  
  Three sources documented:
  
    1. Ledger history (1GE7PUq..., tab 'Ledger history') — what lineage-engine reads today. Quarterly. Authoritative. Includes both manual contributions and GAS-tokenized recurring contributions after the quarterly roll-up.
  
    2. Scored Chatlogs (1Tbj7H5..., tab 'Scored Chatlogs', WHERE col L empty) — daily. Pending governor review. First entry 2024-12-13 (the history floor that the caveat banner has to surface verbatim).
  
    3. Recurring Transactions (1GE7PUq..., tab 'Recurring Transactions', headers row 4) — registry of recurring rewards/charges. Phase 4.2 derives 'pending recurring since column F's Most Recent Tokenization Date' per active recurring entry per contributor — no actual claims submitted, just confidence-grade estimates of what the next quarterly settlement will roll forward.
  
  Dedup is clean: an existing GAS that promotes scored entries to ledger writes the Ledger history row number into Scored Chatlogs column L. A pending entry has col L empty by definition. No fuzzy matching, no timestamp joins — we honor what the GAS already records.
  
  Two surfaces with two doctrines:
  
    - Web credential page (canonical + per-program) shows ALL THREE sources as separately-headed sections with caveat banners + status pills (warm-amber 'pending review', muted-grey 'auto · pending tokenization'). Recent activity surfaces same-day.
  
    - Printed PDF certificate (+ canonical CV PDF) stays canonical-only. Same doctrine as §17.6 / Phase 3b — the printable artifact is frozen and formal; including pending claims would undercut its meaning. The contributor's 'Download PDF' will never produce a snapshot that mixes pending claims with recognized work.
  
  Implementation outline (deferred):
    - lineage-engine: new fetch_pending_chatlogs.py + fetch_recurring_registry.py; build_unified_cv merges into cv['pending_contributions'] + cv['expected_recurring']. HTML renderer reads all three; markdown→PDF reader unchanged.
    - truesight_me: credentials/index.html renders the new sections; per-program program-shell.js inherits via the same CV JSON.
    - Per-program filter for Scored Chatlogs by Project Name fuzzy-match deferred to 4.3 (no native program FK on that sheet).
  
  Open questions captured: section ordering, naming for 'Expected recurring', orphan handling if col L points to a missing Ledger history row. Decided when each sub-phase ships.
  
  PR: TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context#152 (squash-merged 49d14dd).
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
